lesson.menu_desc: Buying tickets and talking about art in Paris -- How much is / How much are + There is / There are -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- A Day at the Museum -- Tickets & Art
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Gabriela Pires | Aula 15 -- A Day at the Museum | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Good morning, and welcome to the museum. The entrance is here, on your right. One adult ticket is twelve euros, and a student ticket is only six euros. If you have a student card, please show it at the entrance. Inside, you can see a beautiful exhibition of French art. On the first floor, there is a famous painting from the eighteen hundreds. It is a masterpiece, and many people come only to see it. On the second floor, there are many sculptures by a great artist. A guide can walk with you and explain each room. Photos are welcome, but please do not use the flash. Enjoy your day, and take your time in every gallery.

Today I visited a museum in Paris, and I bought my ticket in English. At the entrance, I asked, how much is a student ticket? It was only six euros, so I showed my student card. Inside, there was an amazing exhibition of French art. On the first floor, I saw a famous painting. It was so beautiful, a real masterpiece. On the second floor, there were many sculptures by a great artist. A guide explained the rooms to us, and I understood almost everything. I took many photos, but I did not use the flash. I spent three hours in the galleries, and I was so happy. I bought my ticket, asked questions, and talked about art, all in English.

extra_audio.text: First, you go to the entrance and you ask, how much is a ticket? Next, you buy your ticket and you show your student card. After that, you walk into the first gallery and you see the exhibition. Then, you look at the paintings and the sculptures, and a guide explains them. Finally, you take some photos, but you do not use the flash.
